title: pillowy vietnamese cod bao
description: briny, pillowy, kid-friendly vietnamese dish featuring cod, nigiri-shaping, ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly
instructions: Combine sweet rice flour and warm water in a large mixing bowl to form a dough.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Use a rolling pin to flatten each ball into a thin circle.
Heat 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add 2 cod fillets to the skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, until cooked through.
Transfer the cooked cod to a plate and set aside.
Add 1/4 cup of coconut oil to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until melted.
Brush the melted coconut oil onto each bao dough circle.
Place a cooked cod fillet in the center of each bao dough circle.
Fold the dough over the cod to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the bao.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Cook the bao for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Serve the bao hot with pickled carrots and daikon, scallions, and sesame seeds.
Serve with fish sauce and sugar for dipping.
Serve with black pepper for added flavor.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
